questions.questionText: An administrator needs to optimize storage performance for a VDI environment experiencing boot storms. Which Nutanix feature should be enabled?

options.text: Shadow Clones to cache read-only boot disk data locally on each node
Erasure Coding to reduce storage overhead on boot disks
Post-process compression to reduce boot disk I/O
Increase OpLog size to buffer more write operations

questions.explanation: Shadow Clones automatically create read-only local copies of heavily-read vDisks across nodes, eliminating cross-node reads during VDI boot storms. This is specifically designed for VDI use cases.

questions.questionText: An administrator observes high write latency on a storage container handling random write workloads. Which DSF component is primarily responsible for absorbing random writes before destaging to the Extent Store?

options.text: Unified Cache (Content Cache)
OpLog
Curator
Medusa (metadata store)

questions.explanation: The OpLog is a write coalescing buffer on SSD that absorbs random writes, coalescing them before sequentially destaging to the Extent Store. Each Stargate instance has approximately 6 GB of OpLog space.

questions.questionText: An administrator runs `ncli container ls` and notices a container with both compression and Erasure Coding enabled. The container hosts a transactional database with heavy random writes. What should the administrator change first to improve write performance?

options.text: Disable Erasure Coding, as EC-X encoding adds CPU overhead and write amplification on heavy write workloads
Disable compression, since inline compression is incompatible with Erasure Coding
Enable deduplication to offset the Erasure Coding overhead
Increase the Replication Factor from RF2 to RF3 to distribute the write load

questions.explanation: Erasure Coding (EC-X) is designed for space efficiency on cold or read-heavy data. On heavy random write workloads, EC adds significant CPU overhead and write amplification due to re-encoding, making it unsuitable for transactional databases.

questions.questionText: While troubleshooting storage performance, an administrator needs to view detailed Stargate I/O metrics for a specific CVM. Which URL provides direct access to the Stargate service page?

options.text: `http://<CVM_IP>:2009`
`http://<CVM_IP>:2020`
`http://<CVM_IP>:9440`
`http://<CVM_IP>:2010`

questions.explanation: The Stargate service page is accessible on port 2009 of any CVM IP. It provides detailed per-vDisk I/O statistics, OpLog utilization, and extent store metrics for troubleshooting.

questions.questionText: An administrator needs to identify which VM is generating an I/O storm on a cluster. Which Prism Element metric combination is most effective for isolating the offending VM?

options.text: Controller IOPS and controller latency per VM in the VM dashboard
Cluster-wide throughput and CPU usage in the Hardware dashboard
Storage container replication status in the Data Resiliency dashboard
CVM memory utilization and hypervisor CPU in the Host dashboard

questions.explanation: The Prism Element VM dashboard shows per-VM controller IOPS, throughput, and latency. Sorting by IOPS or latency quickly identifies which VM is driving an I/O storm on the cluster.

questions.questionText: An administrator runs `ncc health_checks run_all` and receives a warning about high Extent Store fragmentation on several nodes. Which background service is responsible for addressing this through garbage collection and data compaction?

options.text: Curator
Stargate
Cerebro
Prism

questions.explanation: Curator performs background maintenance tasks including garbage collection, data compaction, extent store defragmentation, and triggering deduplication/compression scans. It coordinates these activities across the cluster.

questions.questionText: A Nutanix cluster has both SSD and HDD tiers. An administrator wants to ensure that frequently accessed data remains on SSDs while infrequently accessed data moves to HDDs automatically. Which mechanism handles this?

options.text: Information Lifecycle Management (ILM) auto-tiering
Manual vDisk migration via `ncli vdisk edit`
OpLog pinning to SSD tier
Curator-triggered Erasure Coding

questions.explanation: ILM (Information Lifecycle Management) automatically monitors data access patterns and moves hot data to the SSD tier while migrating cold data to the HDD tier, optimizing cost and performance without manual intervention.

questions.questionText: An administrator is configuring a storage container for a workload consisting of linked-clone VDI desktops. Which combination of features is most appropriate?

options.text: Fingerprint-based deduplication and Shadow Clones enabled
Erasure Coding with RF3 replication
Inline compression with post-process deduplication disabled
RF2 replication with all data efficiency features disabled

questions.explanation: Linked-clone VDI desktops share common base images, making fingerprint-based deduplication highly effective. Shadow Clones further optimize read performance during boot storms by caching read-only data locally on each node.

questions.questionText: While reviewing a container's configuration, an administrator runs `ncli container ls` and sees `Compression Enabled: true` with `Compression Delay in Seconds: 0`. What does a compression delay of 0 indicate?

options.text: Inline compression is active — data is compressed at write time using LZ4
Compression is disabled and waiting for manual trigger
Post-process compression runs immediately after the write completes
Compression delay is set to the system default of 60 minutes

questions.explanation: A compression delay of 0 means inline compression (LZ4) is enabled, compressing data during the initial write. This is suitable for sequential workloads. Post-process compression uses a delay greater than 0 (commonly 60 minutes).

questions.questionText: An administrator needs to check the total usable storage capacity and current utilization of all storage pools. Which command provides this information?

options.text: `ncli storage-pool ls`
`ncli container ls`
`acli storage.list`
`ncli disk ls`

questions.explanation: `ncli storage-pool ls` lists all storage pools with their total capacity, used space, and available space. Storage pools aggregate physical disks and are the underlying layer for storage containers.

questions.questionText: An administrator notices that a container with post-process deduplication shows high CPU usage on all CVMs during off-peak hours. What is causing this behavior?

options.text: Curator is running deduplication fingerprinting and dedup scans across the cluster
Stargate is re-encoding Erasure Coded data during maintenance windows
The OpLog is being flushed to the Extent Store in bulk
Cerebro is performing remote replication to a DR site

questions.explanation: Post-process deduplication runs as a Curator background task, computing fingerprints and identifying duplicate extents. This is CPU-intensive and typically runs during periods of lower I/O activity, which explains the off-peak CPU spike.

questions.questionText: An administrator wants to determine the replication factor configured on a specific storage container named "SQL-Data." Which command shows this?

options.text: `ncli container ls name=SQL-Data`
`acli container.get SQL-Data`
`ncli vdisk ls container=SQL-Data`
`ncli storage-pool get SQL-Data`

questions.explanation: `ncli container ls name=SQL-Data` displays the container configuration including the replication factor (RF2 or RF3), compression settings, deduplication settings, and Erasure Coding status.

questions.questionText: An administrator is evaluating whether to enable Erasure Coding on a container hosting archived log files that are rarely read or modified. Which statement correctly describes the trade-off?

options.text: EC-X reduces storage overhead compared to RF2 but increases CPU during encoding and is best for cold data
EC-X provides better write performance than RF2 for all workload types
EC-X eliminates the need for replication factor entirely
EC-X can only be used on containers with deduplication disabled

questions.explanation: Erasure Coding (EC-X) trades CPU overhead for storage efficiency by using parity strips instead of full data copies. It is ideal for cold, infrequently modified data like archived logs where the CPU overhead of encoding is acceptable.

questions.questionText: During a storage performance investigation, an administrator accesses the Stargate 2009 page and observes that the OpLog is consistently at 90%+ utilization. What does this indicate?

options.text: Write ingestion rate exceeds the OpLog destage rate to the Extent Store, potentially causing write latency
The Unified Cache is full and spilling over into the OpLog
Deduplication fingerprints are consuming OpLog space
The Extent Store is full and the OpLog is acting as overflow storage

questions.explanation: High OpLog utilization means writes are arriving faster than they can be destaged (flushed) to the Extent Store. This can lead to increased write latency as the OpLog becomes a bottleneck, often indicating undersized SSD capacity or excessive write load.

questions.questionText: An administrator is configuring QoS (Quality of Service) for VMs on a Nutanix cluster. At which level can IOPS throttling be applied?

options.text: Per-VM level through Prism
Per storage container level through `ncli`
Per storage pool level through Prism
Per physical disk through CVM configuration

questions.explanation: Nutanix QoS allows IOPS throttling at the per-VM level, enabling administrators to limit the I/O impact of noisy-neighbor VMs. This is configured through Prism and applies to all vDisks associated with the VM.

questions.questionText: An administrator needs to isolate a high-IOPS analytics workload from a latency-sensitive OLTP database. What is the recommended approach on a Nutanix cluster?

options.text: Place each workload in a separate storage container with appropriate data services (compression, dedup) configured per workload
Create separate storage pools for each workload on the same nodes
Configure VLAN tagging on storage traffic to isolate I/O paths
Use Erasure Coding on the analytics container and RF3 on the OLTP container

questions.explanation: Separate storage containers allow independent configuration of replication factor, compression, deduplication, and Erasure Coding tailored to each workload's profile. Combined with per-VM QoS, this provides effective workload isolation.

questions.questionText: An administrator runs an NCC check and receives a FAIL for `stargate_disk_io_latency_check`. Which next step is most appropriate?

options.text: Access the Stargate 2009 page on the affected CVM to identify which vDisks and physical disks are experiencing high latency
Restart the Stargate service on all CVMs with `genesis restart stargate`
Immediately enable Erasure Coding on all containers to reduce disk I/O
Run `ncli storage-pool ls` to check if the storage pool is full

questions.explanation: The Stargate 2009 page (`http://<CVM_IP>:2009`) provides granular I/O latency metrics per vDisk and per physical disk, allowing the administrator to pinpoint whether the issue is a failing disk, a hot vDisk, or an overloaded node.

questions.questionText: An administrator wants to verify that the Unified Cache (Content Cache) is effectively serving read requests. Where should they look?

options.text: Stargate 2009 page — Content Cache hit rate and eviction statistics
`ncli container ls` — cache utilization per container
Prism Element Hardware dashboard — SSD read/write ratio
`ncc health_checks run_all` — cache health check output

questions.explanation: The Stargate 2009 page provides detailed Content Cache statistics including hit rate, miss rate, evictions, and fingerprint lookups. A low hit rate may indicate the working set exceeds cache size or deduplication is not effective.

questions.questionText: An administrator creates a new storage container and wants to enable deduplication. Which statement about Nutanix deduplication is correct?

options.text: Deduplication is fingerprint-based and runs as a post-process by default, most effective for VDI and clone workloads
Deduplication is always inline and cannot be configured for post-process operation
Deduplication operates at the file system level and requires NTFS or ext4
Deduplication must be enabled at the storage pool level before it can be used on containers

questions.explanation: Nutanix deduplication uses SHA-1 fingerprinting and runs post-process by default. It is most effective for workloads with high data similarity, such as VDI environments with linked clones sharing common base images.

questions.questionText: An administrator runs `ncli container edit name=DB-Prod compression-enabled=true compression-delay=3600`. What is the effect of this configuration?

options.text: Post-process compression is enabled with a 1-hour delay — data written within the last hour remains uncompressed
Inline compression is enabled with a 1-hour timeout before writes are rejected
Compression is scheduled to activate 1 hour after the container is created
The container will compress data using LZ4 only after reaching 3600 IOPS

questions.explanation: A compression delay of 3600 seconds (1 hour) enables post-process compression. Data remains uncompressed for the first hour after being written, then Curator compresses it. This is ideal for random write workloads where data may be overwritten shortly after initial write.

questions.questionText: An administrator is investigating slow VM performance. Using Prism Element, they see the VM's controller latency exceeds 20ms. Which I/O path component should the administrator investigate first?

options.text: Stargate service on the local CVM hosting the VM — check OpLog and Extent Store latency
Cerebro replication lag to the remote DR cluster
Prism Central communication latency to the management plane
Zookeeper metadata store response time

questions.explanation: In the DSF I/O path (VM → vDisk → Stargate → Extent Store → Disk), the Stargate service on the local CVM is the primary handler. High controller latency typically points to OpLog congestion, Extent Store disk latency, or an overwhelmed local CVM.

questions.questionText: An administrator needs to find all vDisks associated with a specific VM named "Oracle-Prod" for I/O analysis. Which approach is most direct?

options.text: View the VM in Prism Element, navigate to the Virtual Disks tab, and note the vDisk UUIDs for correlation with Stargate metrics
Run `ncli storage-pool ls` and filter by VM name
Run `acli net.list` and search for the VM's network attachment
Check the Curator 2010 page for VM-to-vDisk mappings

questions.explanation: Prism Element's VM detail page shows all attached virtual disks with their vDisk identifiers, container association, size, and I/O statistics. These UUIDs can be cross-referenced on the Stargate 2009 page for deeper analysis.

questions.questionText: An administrator is planning to migrate a workload from RF2 to RF3 on an existing container. What impact should they expect?

options.text: Increased write amplification — each write produces three copies instead of two, requiring more SSD/HDD capacity and increased network bandwidth
Decreased read latency because RF3 distributes reads across three replicas
No impact on capacity because RF3 uses parity instead of full copies
The container must be emptied and recreated since RF cannot be changed in-place

questions.explanation: RF3 stores three full copies of each extent, increasing write amplification (every write must be committed to three nodes), consuming ~50% more raw capacity than RF2, and generating additional network traffic between CVMs.

questions.questionText: An administrator notices that a 4-node cluster with all-SSD storage has no HDD tier. How does ILM (Information Lifecycle Management) behave in this scenario?

options.text: ILM has no tiering effect since all storage is on a single tier — data remains on SSD
ILM automatically creates a virtual HDD tier using reserved SSD capacity
ILM moves cold data to the OpLog for long-term storage
ILM is disabled and must be manually re-enabled when HDDs are added

questions.explanation: ILM auto-tiering moves data between SSD and HDD tiers based on access patterns. On an all-SSD (all-flash) cluster, there is no HDD tier to migrate cold data to, so ILM tiering has no operational effect.

questions.questionText: An administrator is troubleshooting why Shadow Clones are not activating for a VDI container. Which condition must be met for Shadow Clones to trigger?

options.text: The vDisk must be read from multiple nodes and the read-to-write ratio must be heavily read-dominant
Deduplication must be disabled on the container
The VM must be powered off before Shadow Clone creation
Shadow Clones require Erasure Coding to be enabled on the container

questions.explanation: Shadow Clones trigger automatically when a vDisk is heavily read from multiple nodes with minimal writes. The feature creates read-only local copies on requesting nodes, reducing cross-node I/O. Any write to the vDisk invalidates existing Shadow Clones.

questions.questionText: An administrator wants to check the current Erasure Coding savings on a container named "Archive-Data." Which command shows EC strip savings?

options.text: `ncli container ls name=Archive-Data` and review the Erasure Coding status and savings fields
`acli ec.status container=Archive-Data`
`ncc health_checks ec_savings_check`
`ncli storage-pool get name=Archive-Data`

questions.explanation: The `ncli container ls name=<name>` output includes Erasure Coding configuration status and savings metrics, showing how much space EC has saved compared to full replication on that specific container.

questions.questionText: An administrator is analyzing the DSF I/O path and wants to understand where a write operation is persisted first. What is the correct order for a write in the Nutanix I/O path?

options.text: VM → Stargate → OpLog (local SSD) → acknowledge to VM → destage to Extent Store
VM → Stargate → Extent Store (HDD) → acknowledge to VM → copy to OpLog
VM → Unified Cache → OpLog → Extent Store → acknowledge to VM
VM → Curator → Stargate → OpLog → acknowledge to VM

questions.explanation: Nutanix uses the OpLog as a write-ahead log. Writes are first persisted to the OpLog on local SSD (and replicated OpLog on a remote CVM for RF2/RF3), then acknowledged to the VM. Destaging to the Extent Store happens asynchronously.

questions.questionText: An administrator sees high read latency on a container with deduplication enabled. The Stargate 2009 page shows a low Content Cache hit rate. What is the most likely cause?

options.text: The working set exceeds the Unified Cache (Content Cache) size, causing frequent cache evictions and reads from disk
Deduplication is consuming too much OpLog space
Erasure Coding is interfering with the deduplication fingerprints
The Curator scan is holding a lock on the Content Cache

questions.explanation: The Unified Cache (Content Cache) stores deduplicated fingerprinted data for read acceleration. If the working set is larger than the available SSD cache, evictions increase and reads must be served from the Extent Store on disk, raising latency.

questions.questionText: An administrator runs `ncc health_checks run_all` and receives a warning about CVM memory pressure on one node. How does this affect storage performance?

options.text: Stargate performance degrades because it relies on CVM memory for caching, metadata, and I/O processing
Only Prism web console performance is affected; storage I/O is unaffected
CVM memory pressure only impacts Curator background tasks
The CVM automatically live-migrates its workload to another CVM

questions.explanation: The CVM runs Stargate (and other storage services) which depend heavily on memory for the Unified Cache, OpLog management, and metadata lookups. Memory pressure on the CVM directly degrades storage I/O performance for all VMs on that host.

questions.questionText: An administrator wants to enable inline compression on a new container. Which compression algorithm does Nutanix use for inline compression?

options.text: LZ4 — a fast, low-CPU-overhead compression algorithm suitable for inline operation
GZIP — providing maximum compression ratio at higher CPU cost
Snappy — used exclusively for post-process compression
ZSTD — the default inline algorithm since AOS 6.0

questions.explanation: Nutanix uses LZ4 for inline compression due to its low latency and minimal CPU overhead, making it suitable for compressing data in the write path without significantly impacting I/O performance.

questions.questionText: An administrator is troubleshooting a specific vDisk that shows consistently high latency. They need to find the vDisk's NFS file path for advanced debugging. Which location on the CVM filesystem reflects the vDisk namespace?

options.text: `/home/nutanix/data/stargate-storage/disks/` — the NFS datastore path where vDisks are presented
`/etc/nutanix/stargate/vdisks/`
`/var/log/stargate/vdisk_map/`
`/home/nutanix/config/vdisk_list.json`

questions.explanation: Nutanix DSF presents vDisks via NFS to the hypervisor. The Stargate storage directory on the CVM contains the mounted datastore paths that map to vDisks, enabling advanced troubleshooting and direct vDisk-level analysis.

questions.questionText: An administrator receives an alert in Prism that a container is approaching 90% utilization. Which proactive measure should they take to avoid performance degradation?

options.text: Enable or review data efficiency features (compression, dedup, EC) and consider expanding the cluster or removing stale snapshots
Disable Replication Factor to immediately reclaim 50% of capacity
Move all VMs to a different cluster and delete the container
Increase the OpLog size to accommodate more data before destaging

questions.explanation: Approaching capacity limits degrades performance due to reduced free space for OpLog, Curator operations, and new writes. Enabling compression/dedup or EC can reclaim space, while removing stale snapshots and planning cluster expansion address root capacity.

questions.questionText: An administrator wants to measure the actual data reduction ratio achieved by deduplication and compression on a container. Where is this metric displayed?

options.text: Prism Element Storage dashboard — container details showing pre-reduction vs. post-reduction usage and savings ratio
`ncc health_checks data_reduction_check`
`acli container.dedup_stats`
The Curator 2010 page — global dedup ratio only

questions.explanation: Prism Element's Storage dashboard shows per-container data reduction metrics including the overall savings ratio from combined deduplication and compression, as well as individual contributions of each feature.

questions.questionText: An administrator has a mixed workload cluster with both VDI desktops and a SQL database. The SQL database requires consistent low-latency I/O. Which approach best protects the database from VDI I/O variability?

options.text: Apply per-VM QoS IOPS limits on VDI desktop VMs and place the SQL database in a dedicated storage container
Enable Erasure Coding on the VDI container to reduce VDI IOPS
Disable the OpLog on the SQL database container to bypass the write buffer
Configure Shadow Clones on the SQL database for read optimization

questions.explanation: Per-VM QoS throttles VDI VMs to a maximum IOPS, preventing them from consuming all storage bandwidth. A dedicated container for SQL ensures independent data services tuning, and combined these isolate the latency-sensitive workload.

questions.questionText: An administrator runs `ncli container ls` and notices that a container has both deduplication and Erasure Coding enabled. Is this a supported configuration?

options.text: Yes — deduplication and Erasure Coding can coexist on the same container; dedup runs first, then EC encodes the deduplicated extents
No — enabling both features simultaneously causes data corruption
No — the system automatically disables dedup when EC is enabled
Yes, but only on all-SSD clusters with RF3

questions.explanation: Nutanix supports enabling both deduplication and Erasure Coding on the same container. Deduplication reduces unique data first, then Erasure Coding provides space-efficient protection for the remaining unique extents, maximizing overall savings.

questions.questionText: An administrator is investigating why write performance dropped after enabling inline compression on a container with random write workloads. What should they change?

options.text: Switch from inline compression (delay=0) to post-process compression (delay>0) to avoid compressing data that may be overwritten quickly
Enable Erasure Coding to offset the compression overhead
Increase the CVM CPU allocation from 4 to 8 vCPUs
Disable the Unified Cache to free SSD resources for the OpLog

questions.explanation: Inline compression processes every write through LZ4, adding latency to the write path. For random workloads where data is frequently overwritten, post-process compression is more efficient because it only compresses data that persists beyond the configured delay.

questions.questionText: An administrator needs to check disk-level health and identify any failed or degrading disks in the cluster. Which command provides per-disk status?

options.text: `ncli disk ls` — shows all disks with their status, tier (SSD/HDD), and health information
`acli disk.list` — shows disk I/O statistics
`ncc health_checks disk_usage_check` — shows only capacity usage
`ncli storage-pool ls` — shows per-disk health within pools

questions.explanation: `ncli disk ls` displays a comprehensive list of all physical disks in the cluster including their online/offline status, tier assignment (SSD or HDD), serial numbers, and CVM association, essential for hardware troubleshooting.

questions.questionText: An administrator is reviewing Prism analytics and notices that one CVM consistently has higher IOPS than others. All hosts have similar VM counts. What should the administrator investigate?

options.text: Whether data locality is maintained — VMs may be running on hosts whose data resides on remote CVMs, causing cross-node I/O to load that CVM
Whether the CVM has a larger OpLog allocation
Whether Prism Central is directing more API calls to that CVM
Whether the CVM has additional physical NICs

questions.explanation: Nutanix DSF prefers local reads/writes (data locality). If VMs were migrated without their data following, I/O is served remotely by the CVM that owns the data. This creates uneven CVM load and should be investigated via data locality metrics.

questions.questionText: An administrator wants to run a comprehensive NCC health check focusing only on storage-related checks. Which approach is most targeted?

options.text: `ncc health_checks run_all` and filter the results for storage-related check categories
`ncli cluster health-check run type=storage`
`acli storage.health_check run_all`
Run `ncc --help` and execute individual storage check plugins one at a time

questions.explanation: NCC (Nutanix Cluster Check) runs all health checks with `ncc health_checks run_all`. While there is no single storage-only flag, results are categorized and can be filtered. Individual storage checks like `disk_io_latency_check` can also be run separately.

questions.questionText: An administrator needs to understand how many extent groups are stored on each disk for capacity planning. Which background service maintains this metadata and manages extent group placement?

options.text: Stargate — manages extent groups on local disks and coordinates with Medusa for metadata tracking
Curator — exclusively manages extent group placement and rebalancing
Cerebro — handles extent group replication across sites
Prism — provides extent group placement through the REST API

questions.explanation: Stargate is the primary I/O manager that creates and manages extent groups (typically 1 MB extents) on local disks. Medusa (the distributed metadata store) tracks extent group locations, while Curator handles background optimization tasks.

questions.questionText: An administrator needs to list all virtual networks configured on an AHV cluster. Which command should they use?

options.text: `acli net.list`
`ncli network list`
`ovs-vsctl list-nets`
`acli vm.net_list`

questions.explanation: `acli net.list` displays all virtual networks (both VLAN-backed and overlay) configured on the AHV cluster, including their UUIDs, VLAN IDs, and associated virtual switch information.

questions.questionText: An administrator needs to view detailed configuration of a network named "Production-VLAN100" including its VLAN ID and IP pool settings. Which command provides this?

options.text: `acli net.get Production-VLAN100`
`ncli network get name=Production-VLAN100`
`ovs-vsctl show Production-VLAN100`
`acli vm.net_get Production-VLAN100`

questions.explanation: `acli net.get <network_name>` displays detailed network configuration including the VLAN ID, virtual switch assignment, IPAM settings (IP pool, gateway, DNS), and associated subnet information.

questions.questionText: An administrator is configuring an AHV host and needs to verify the current Open vSwitch bridge configuration, including attached ports and interfaces. Which command should they run on the AHV host?

options.text: `ovs-vsctl show`
`acli net.list`
`manage_ovs --show_bridges`
`ncli host ls`

questions.explanation: `ovs-vsctl show` displays the complete OVS configuration on the AHV host, including bridges (e.g., br0), bond interfaces, uplink ports, and their VLAN configurations. It is the primary OVS diagnostic command.

questions.questionText: An administrator wants to check the bond status of network interfaces on an AHV host to verify uplink redundancy. Which command provides bond member status and active link information?

options.text: `manage_ovs --bridge_status` or `ovs-appctl bond/show`
`acli host.nic_status`
`ncli host list-nics`
`ovs-vsctl list-bonds`

questions.explanation: `manage_ovs --bridge_status` provides a summary of bond configuration and link status. For detailed bond information including individual slave states, `ovs-appctl bond/show <bond_name>` shows active/backup status and hash distribution.

questions.questionText: An administrator is setting up a new AHV cluster and needs to choose a bond mode for a workload requiring maximum throughput using switch-independent bonding. Which bond mode should they select?

options.text: balance-slb — hash-based load balancing that does not require switch configuration
active-backup — only one NIC active at a time, no load balancing
LACP (802.3ad) — requires switch-side LACP configuration
balance-tcp — requires physical switch support for port channel

questions.explanation: balance-slb (Source Load Balancing) distributes traffic across multiple uplinks based on MAC address hashing without requiring any physical switch configuration, making it ideal when maximum throughput is needed with switch-independent operation.

questions.questionText: An administrator configures LACP (802.3ad) bond mode on an AHV host. What additional configuration is required?

options.text: The physical switch ports connected to the AHV host must also be configured with LACP in an appropriate port channel or LAG
Each VM must be configured with a dedicated physical NIC
Jumbo frames must be enabled cluster-wide before LACP can be activated
Prism Central must be deployed to manage LACP bonds

questions.explanation: LACP (Link Aggregation Control Protocol / 802.3ad) requires both ends to participate. The physical switch ports must be configured in an LACP LAG (Link Aggregation Group) that matches the AHV host's bond configuration.

questions.questionText: An administrator wants to enable jumbo frames for storage traffic between CVMs. What MTU value should be configured and where?

options.text: Set MTU to 9000 on the AHV host virtual switch uplinks, the physical switch ports, and CVM network interfaces
Set MTU to 9000 only on the CVM network interfaces
Set MTU to 1500 on the virtual switch and 9000 on the physical switch
Configure MTU 9000 only in Prism Central's network settings

questions.explanation: Jumbo frames (MTU 9000) must be configured end-to-end: AHV host vSwitch uplinks, all intermediate physical switch ports, and CVM network interfaces. A mismatch at any point causes fragmentation or dropped frames.

questions.questionText: An administrator needs to verify that VLAN tagging is correctly configured for VM traffic on VLAN 200. The VMs can reach each other but not the default gateway. What should they check first?

options.text: Verify the physical switch trunk port allows VLAN 200 and that the gateway interface is configured on VLAN 200
Run `acli vm.list` to check if VMs are powered on
Restart the OVS service on all AHV hosts with `systemctl restart openvswitch`
Check if Erasure Coding is impacting network throughput

questions.explanation: When VMs on the same VLAN communicate but cannot reach the gateway, the issue is typically at the physical network layer — either the switch trunk port does not include the VLAN or the Layer 3 gateway is not configured for that VLAN.

questions.questionText: An administrator is deploying Flow Virtual Networking and needs to create an isolated network segment using Geneve encapsulation. Which construct should they create?

options.text: A VPC (Virtual Private Cloud) with overlay subnets in Prism Central
A VLAN-backed network with `acli net.create` on each AHV host
An OVS bridge with `ovs-vsctl add-br` on the AHV host
A storage container network with `ncli container create`

questions.explanation: VPCs (Virtual Private Clouds) in Flow Virtual Networking create isolated network segments using Geneve overlay encapsulation. VPCs are configured in Prism Central and support overlay subnets, routing, and network isolation.

questions.questionText: An administrator configures a VPC in Prism Central and creates an overlay subnet. VMs on this subnet need external internet access. Which Flow Virtual Networking feature enables this?

options.text: Floating IPs — providing NAT-based external access for VMs within the VPC
Shadow Clones — enabling local network caching on each node
OVS port mirroring — forwarding traffic to an external firewall
LACP bonds — aggregating links for external connectivity

questions.explanation: Floating IPs provide NAT-based external access for VMs in a VPC. A floating IP is associated with a VM's private overlay IP and translated at the VPC's external gateway, enabling outbound and inbound connectivity.

questions.questionText: An administrator needs to connect a VPC to an on-premises data center network. Which Flow Virtual Networking component facilitates this connectivity?

options.text: VPN gateway — establishing a tunnel between the VPC and the external network
VLAN trunk — extending the physical VLAN into the VPC
OVS bond — bridging the VPC overlay to a physical NIC
Floating IP — routing all VPC traffic through a single NAT address

questions.explanation: VPN gateways in Flow Virtual Networking establish secure tunnels between VPCs and external networks, enabling connectivity to on-premises data centers or other cloud environments while maintaining VPC isolation.

questions.questionText: An administrator is configuring Flow Network Security (microsegmentation) and needs to group VMs by application tier. What mechanism does Nutanix use for VM grouping?

options.text: Categories — key-value tags assigned to VMs (e.g., AppTier:Web, AppTier:DB)
OVS port groups — assigning VMs to specific switch ports
Storage containers — grouping VMs by their storage assignment
VLAN IDs — placing VMs on the same VLAN for group membership

questions.explanation: Nutanix Flow uses Categories (key-value pairs) to group VMs logically. Categories like AppTier:Web, AppTier:App, AppTier:DB allow security policies to reference groups of VMs regardless of their network or host placement.

questions.questionText: An administrator creates a security policy in Flow Network Security for a three-tier application. They want to allow web tier VMs to communicate with app tier VMs on port 8080, but block direct web-to-database traffic. Which policy type should they use?

options.text: Application security policy — defining specific allowed flows between category-based tiers
Isolation policy — blocking all traffic between the web and database categories
Quarantine policy — isolating the database VMs from all traffic
VPC default policy — blocking all inter-VPC traffic

questions.explanation: Application security policies allow granular definition of permitted traffic flows between VM categories. The administrator can specify that AppTier:Web may communicate with AppTier:App on port 8080, while all other flows (including web-to-DB) are denied.

questions.questionText: An administrator wants to block all communication between VMs in the "Development" category and VMs in the "Production" category. Which Flow policy type is designed for this?

options.text: Isolation policy — blocking traffic between two specified categories
Application policy — defining allowed ports between categories
Quarantine policy — isolating compromised VMs
Default gateway policy — filtering inter-VLAN traffic

questions.explanation: Isolation policies are specifically designed to block all traffic between two categories. By creating an isolation policy between Environment:Development and Environment:Production, all cross-environment traffic is denied.

questions.questionText: A security team has identified a compromised VM and needs to immediately restrict its network access while allowing forensic analysis tools to connect. Which Flow policy should be applied?

options.text: Quarantine policy — placing the VM in strict or forensic quarantine mode
Isolation policy — isolating the VM from all categories
Application policy — removing the VM from all allowed flows
Delete the VM's virtual NIC to disconnect it from the network

questions.explanation: The Quarantine policy in Flow provides two modes: Strict (blocks all traffic) and Forensic (blocks general traffic but allows specific forensic tool access). Quarantine policies have the highest priority and override all other security policies.

questions.questionText: An administrator creates a new application security policy in Flow and wants to observe the traffic patterns before enforcing the rules. Which mode should the policy be set to initially?

options.text: Monitor mode — logs traffic that would be affected without actually enforcing the rules
Apply mode — enforces rules immediately with logging
Simulation mode — creates a virtual network copy for testing
Audit mode — sends all policy decisions to a SIEM

questions.explanation: Monitor mode allows administrators to observe which traffic flows would be allowed or denied by the policy without actually enforcing the rules. This enables validation that the policy will not break legitimate application traffic before switching to Apply mode.

questions.questionText: An administrator has four Flow security policies affecting the same VM: a quarantine policy, an isolation policy, an application policy, and the default policy. In what order are these policies evaluated?

options.text: Quarantine → Isolation → Application → Default
Application → Isolation → Quarantine → Default
Default → Application → Isolation → Quarantine
All policies are evaluated simultaneously with no priority

questions.explanation: Flow security policy priority order is: Quarantine (highest) → Isolation → Application → Default (lowest). If a VM is quarantined, the quarantine policy takes precedence over all others, ensuring compromised VM containment.

questions.questionText: An administrator wants to visualize the actual network traffic flows between VMs in Prism Central before creating security policies. Which Flow feature provides this capability?

options.text: Flow visualization in Prism Central — showing discovered traffic flows between categories and VMs
`ovs-ofctl dump-flows` on each AHV host
`acli net.traffic_stats` on the cluster
NCC network flow analysis check

questions.explanation: Prism Central's Flow visualization discovers and displays actual network traffic between VMs and categories, helping administrators understand existing communication patterns before designing and implementing security policies.

questions.questionText: An administrator needs to troubleshoot OVS flow rules on an AHV host to determine why traffic between two VMs is being dropped. Which command displays the active OpenFlow rules?

options.text: `ovs-ofctl dump-flows br0`
`acli net.flow_rules`
`manage_ovs --list_flows`
`ncli network flow-rules list`

questions.explanation: `ovs-ofctl dump-flows br0` shows all active OpenFlow rules on the br0 bridge, including match criteria and actions. This is essential for diagnosing traffic forwarding decisions, VLAN tagging, and microsegmentation enforcement at the OVS level.

questions.questionText: An administrator needs to test network connectivity from a CVM to another CVM on a different host to validate storage inter-node communication. Which approach is most appropriate?

options.text: SSH to the CVM and use `ping` with the target CVM's backplane IP address to verify Layer 3 connectivity
Run `acli vm.ping` from Prism Element to test inter-CVM connectivity
Use `ncli cluster ping` to test all node-to-node communication
Check the Cerebro 2020 page for inter-node latency metrics

questions.explanation: Direct connectivity tests from a CVM using standard tools (ping, iperf, netcat) against other CVMs' backplane IPs validate the storage network path. This tests the same network path that Stargate uses for cross-node I/O.

questions.questionText: An administrator is configuring the default virtual switch (vs0) on an AHV cluster and needs to assign two physical NICs to the uplink. Which tool should they use?

options.text: `manage_ovs` — the Nutanix utility for modifying virtual switch and bond configuration on AHV hosts
`ovs-vsctl add-port` — directly adding ports to the OVS bridge
`acli vs.update` — modifying virtual switch via the AHV CLI
Prism Element → Network Configuration → Physical NIC assignment

questions.explanation: The `manage_ovs` utility is the supported Nutanix tool for configuring virtual switch uplinks, bond modes, and MTU on AHV hosts. Direct OVS commands (`ovs-vsctl`) should not be used as they may be overwritten by the Nutanix management layer.

questions.questionText: An administrator sets up a VLAN-backed network with `acli net.create VLAN200 vlan=200 vswitch_name=vs0 ip_config=10.10.200.0/24`. What does the `ip_config` parameter configure?

options.text: IPAM-managed subnet — enabling AHV-managed DHCP with the specified subnet for VMs on this network
A static IP assigned to the virtual switch itself
The CVM's IP address on VLAN 200
The default gateway for the physical switch's VLAN 200 interface

questions.explanation: The `ip_config` parameter enables Nutanix-managed IPAM (IP Address Management) for the network, providing DHCP services to VMs. The administrator can further define IP pools within this subnet for automatic VM IP assignment.

questions.questionText: An administrator discovers that VMs on the same AHV host and same VLAN can communicate, but VMs on different hosts on the same VLAN cannot. What is the most likely cause?

options.text: The physical switch trunk ports are not configured to allow the VLAN between hosts
Flow microsegmentation is blocking inter-host traffic
The OVS bridge is misconfigured on one host
Shadow Clones are consuming the network bandwidth

questions.explanation: Intra-host traffic stays within the local OVS bridge, but inter-host traffic traverses the physical network. If physical switch trunk ports do not carry the VLAN, frames are dropped between hosts while local switching continues to work.

questions.questionText: An administrator is comparing overlay subnets and VLAN-backed subnets in Flow Virtual Networking. Which statement is correct?

options.text: Overlay subnets use Geneve encapsulation and do not require VLAN provisioning on the physical switch; VLAN-backed subnets require physical switch configuration
VLAN-backed subnets use Geneve encapsulation for better isolation
Overlay subnets require dedicated physical NICs separate from VLAN-backed networks
Both subnet types require identical physical switch configuration

questions.explanation: Overlay subnets encapsulate traffic using Geneve protocol over the existing physical network, eliminating the need for VLAN provisioning on physical switches. VLAN-backed subnets directly map to physical VLANs and require switch-side trunk configuration.

questions.questionText: An administrator needs to verify the current bond mode configured on an AHV host's virtual switch. Which sequence of commands provides the most complete information?

options.text: `manage_ovs --bridge_status` to see the configured bond mode, then `ovs-appctl bond/show <bond_name>` for detailed slave status
`acli host.bond_mode` to check the current mode
`ncli host ls` and check the NIC bonding column
`cat /etc/sysconfig/network-scripts/ifcfg-bond0` for the bond configuration file

questions.explanation: `manage_ovs --bridge_status` shows the high-level bond configuration including the current mode (active-backup, balance-slb, or LACP). `ovs-appctl bond/show` provides detailed per-slave status, hash distribution, and failover information.

questions.questionText: An administrator wants to change the bond mode from active-backup to balance-slb on a 4-node AHV cluster. What is the impact during the change?

options.text: Brief network disruption on the host being reconfigured as the bond is recreated; other hosts are unaffected if changed one at a time
Cluster-wide network outage requiring all VMs to be powered off
No impact — bond mode changes are applied hot with zero disruption
The host must be removed from the cluster before the bond mode can be changed

questions.explanation: Bond mode changes using `manage_ovs` cause a brief disruption on the individual host as the OVS bond is reconfigured. Performing the change one host at a time minimizes cluster impact, and VMs may experience momentary connectivity loss.

questions.questionText: An administrator creates a VPC and configures two overlay subnets: Subnet-A (10.1.1.0/24) and Subnet-B (10.1.2.0/24). Can VMs on these subnets communicate with each other?

options.text: Yes — subnets within the same VPC can route to each other through the VPC's internal router
No — overlay subnets are always isolated from each other
Only if a floating IP is assigned to each VM
Only if a VPN gateway is configured between the subnets

questions.explanation: VPCs include an internal virtual router that handles routing between subnets within the same VPC. VMs on different overlay subnets in the same VPC can communicate without additional configuration, similar to inter-VLAN routing.

questions.questionText: An administrator is troubleshooting a Flow security policy that appears to be blocking legitimate traffic. The policy is in Apply mode. What is the safest first step?

options.text: Switch the policy from Apply mode to Monitor mode to stop enforcement while continuing to log traffic decisions
Delete the security policy to immediately restore connectivity
Remove all VMs from their categories to bypass the policy
Restart the Flow service on all CVMs

questions.explanation: Switching from Apply to Monitor mode immediately stops enforcement while continuing to log what would be blocked or allowed. This restores connectivity for troubleshooting without losing the policy configuration.

questions.questionText: An administrator needs to configure a security policy that allows SSH (port 22) access only from the "Admin-Tools" category to all VMs in the "Production" category. Which Flow policy element accomplishes this?

options.text: An inbound rule in the application security policy for the Production category, specifying Admin-Tools as the source and TCP port 22 as the allowed service
An isolation policy between Admin-Tools and Production with SSH as an exception
A quarantine policy with SSH port 22 exemption
A VLAN ACL on the physical switch for the Production VLAN

questions.explanation: Application security policies define granular inbound/outbound rules. By adding an inbound rule to the Production policy with Admin-Tools as the source category and TCP/22 as the service, only SSH from Admin-Tools VMs is permitted.

questions.questionText: An administrator configures Flow Network Security and wants to understand what happens to traffic not explicitly matched by any security policy. What is the default behavior?

options.text: The default policy determines the behavior — it can be configured to allow all, deny all, or allow certain traffic categories
All unmatched traffic is silently dropped
All unmatched traffic is allowed without logging
Unmatched traffic triggers an automatic quarantine of the source VM

questions.explanation: The default policy in Flow acts as the fallback for unmatched traffic. Administrators can configure it to allow all traffic (permissive) or deny all (restrictive), with the ability to add specific exceptions within the default policy.

questions.questionText: An administrator needs to verify that VMs tagged with the category "AppTier:Web" are correctly assigned. Which Prism Central section shows all VMs with a specific category assignment?

options.text: Prism Central → Categories → select the category value → view associated entities
`acli vm.list category=AppTier:Web`
`ncli category ls name=AppTier:Web`
Prism Element → VM dashboard → filter by VLAN tag

questions.explanation: In Prism Central, navigating to the Categories section and selecting a specific category value displays all associated entities (VMs, subnets, etc.), providing a clear view of category membership for policy validation.

questions.questionText: An administrator is deploying a multi-tier application and needs to create categories for Web, App, and DB tiers. Where are categories created and managed?

options.text: Prism Central — categories are a Prism Central feature used for entity grouping across clusters
Prism Element — categories are per-cluster and managed locally
AHV CLI — `acli category.create` on each host
CVM CLI — `ncli category create` on the cluster

questions.explanation: Categories are created and managed in Prism Central, which provides a centralized view across all registered clusters. This enables consistent policy application across multiple clusters from a single management point.

questions.questionText: An administrator is comparing the default bond mode (active-backup) with LACP for a latency-sensitive application. Which characteristic is true of active-backup?

options.text: Only one NIC is active at a time — no load balancing, but failover is automatic and requires no switch configuration
Active-backup distributes traffic across all NICs using round-robin
Active-backup requires LACP negotiation with the physical switch
Active-backup provides higher throughput than balance-slb

questions.explanation: Active-backup mode uses a single active NIC while the backup NIC(s) remain standby. Failover is automatic when the active link fails. It provides simplicity and reliability with no switch-side requirements, but no load balancing.

questions.questionText: An administrator needs to verify that a specific VM is correctly connected to the VLAN 300 network. Which Prism Element section shows the VM's NIC configuration including VLAN assignment?

options.text: Prism Element → VM details → NICs tab — showing network attachment, VLAN, MAC address, and IP
`ncli vm ls` — showing VLAN assignment in the output
`ovs-vsctl show` — listing VM port-to-VLAN mappings
Prism Element → Network Configuration → VLAN Members

questions.explanation: The VM details page in Prism Element shows each vNIC's connected network (which includes VLAN assignment), MAC address, and discovered IP address. This is the quickest way to verify a VM's network configuration.

questions.questionText: An administrator sets up Flow Virtual Networking and creates a VPC with an external subnet for floating IPs. VMs with floating IPs still cannot reach the internet. What should they verify?

options.text: The external subnet has correct gateway configuration and the physical network can route traffic from the external subnet to the internet
The VPC's internal router has a default route to the internet
Each VM has LACP bonding enabled for external connectivity
The CVM has a floating IP assigned for NAT translation

questions.explanation: Floating IPs translate VM overlay IPs to addresses on the external subnet. If VMs cannot reach the internet, the external subnet's gateway must be correctly configured and the physical upstream network must have routes for that subnet.

questions.questionText: An administrator runs `ovs-ofctl dump-flows br0` on an AHV host and sees a large number of flow entries with microsegmentation-related actions. What generates these flow entries?

options.text: Flow Network Security (microsegmentation) policies — Prism Central pushes OpenFlow rules to the OVS on each AHV host
LACP bond negotiation messages
CVM storage traffic replication rules
Prism Element VM migration flow entries

questions.explanation: When Flow Network Security policies are applied, Prism Central translates the category-based rules into OpenFlow entries and pushes them to the OVS bridge on each AHV host. These entries enforce microsegmentation at the virtual switch level.

questions.questionText: An administrator is troubleshooting intermittent packet drops between two VMs on different AHV hosts. Both VMs are on the same VLAN with no Flow policies applied. Which diagnostic sequence is most appropriate?

options.text: Check `ovs-vsctl show` for bridge/VLAN config, verify bond status with `manage_ovs --bridge_status`, then check physical switch port errors
Run `ncc health_checks run_all` and wait for storage-related checks
Enable Erasure Coding to reduce network overhead
Restart Stargate on both CVMs to reset network buffers

questions.explanation: Intermittent drops between hosts suggest a physical layer or OVS configuration issue. Verifying the OVS bridge config, bond health, and physical switch port error counters systematically isolates whether the problem is virtual or physical.

questions.questionText: An administrator needs to verify that Geneve encapsulation overhead is not causing MTU-related issues for VMs on overlay subnets. What is the typical overhead added by Geneve encapsulation?

options.text: Approximately 50–54 bytes — the outer Ethernet, IP, UDP, and Geneve headers reduce the effective VM MTU
Exactly 4 bytes — only the VLAN tag overhead
Zero bytes — Geneve uses header compression
1500 bytes — Geneve doubles the frame size

questions.explanation: Geneve encapsulation adds approximately 50–54 bytes of overhead (outer Ethernet 14 + outer IP 20 + UDP 8 + Geneve 8+ bytes). If the physical MTU is 1500, VMs on overlay subnets have an effective MTU of approximately 1446–1450 unless jumbo frames are configured.

questions.questionText: An administrator wants to implement a zero-trust security model for a multi-tier application in Prism Central. What is the recommended approach using Flow?

options.text: Create an application security policy in Monitor mode with explicit allow rules between required tiers, verify traffic in monitoring, then switch to Apply mode
Create isolation policies between every pair of categories immediately
Deploy a quarantine policy for all VMs and whitelist traffic as needed
Configure VLAN ACLs on the physical switch and mirror rules in Flow

questions.explanation: The recommended approach is to create an application policy defining only the explicitly required flows between tiers. Starting in Monitor mode validates that the policy permits all legitimate traffic before enforcing with Apply mode, preventing accidental outages.

questions.questionText: An administrator notices that after enabling Flow Network Security, CVM-to-CVM communication for storage replication has been disrupted. What is the most likely cause and resolution?

options.text: The security policy is inadvertently matching CVM traffic — CVM traffic should be excluded from microsegmentation policies by ensuring CVMs are not assigned to affected categories
Flow automatically blocks all CVM traffic when enabled
The quarantine policy has been applied to all CVMs by default
OVS flow rules for CVM traffic must be manually created with `ovs-ofctl add-flow`

questions.explanation: CVM traffic is critical for cluster operations (storage replication, metadata, etc.) and should never be subject to microsegmentation policies. If CVMs are inadvertently categorized, Flow rules can block essential inter-CVM communication. Ensure CVMs are excluded from all category assignments.

questions.questionText: An administrator needs to check the Cassandra ring status on a Nutanix cluster experiencing metadata read latency. Which command should be run from the CVM?

options.text: nodetool ring
ncli cluster info
zeus_config_printer
curator_cli display_data

questions.explanation: The "nodetool ring" command shows the Cassandra (Medusa) ring status, including node health and token distribution. Cassandra handles metadata operations, and ring issues can cause metadata read latency.

questions.questionText: An administrator wants to retrieve a list of all VMs running on the AHV cluster, including their power states and host assignments. Which command accomplishes this?

options.text: ncli vm ls
acli vm.list
nuclei vm.list
zeus_config_printer --type=vm

questions.explanation: The "acli vm.list" command is the AHV CLI tool for listing all VMs with their power states, UUIDs, and host assignments. ncli does not have a direct "vm ls" subcommand for AHV VM enumeration.

questions.questionText: A Nutanix administrator needs to create a new VM via the v3 REST API on Prism Central. Which HTTP method and endpoint should be used?

options.text: PUT https://prism-central:9440/api/nutanix/v3/vms/{uuid}
POST https://prism-central:9440/api/nutanix/v3/vms
POST https://prism-central:9440/api/nutanix/v2.0/vms
GET https://prism-central:9440/api/nutanix/v3/vms/list

questions.explanation: Creating a new VM with the v3 intent-based API requires a POST request to the /vms endpoint. PUT is used for updates (requires an existing UUID), v2.0 is the legacy API, and GET /vms/list retrieves existing VMs.

questions.questionText: After a power outage, a CVM fails to start the Stargate service. The administrator confirms all disks are online. What is the correct command to restart only the Stargate service?

options.text: cluster start
genesis stop stargate && genesis start stargate
systemctl restart stargate
ncli cluster start-service name=stargate

questions.explanation: Individual CVM services are managed through Genesis. The "genesis stop stargate && genesis start stargate" sequence restarts only the Stargate (storage I/O) service without affecting other services. "cluster start" would restart all services cluster-wide.

questions.questionText: An administrator needs to assign a VM to always run on a specific host for licensing compliance. Which acli command creates this host affinity rule?

options.text: acli vm.affinity_set <vm_name> host_list=<host_ip>
acli vm.update <vm_name> preferred_host=<host_ip>
acli vm.migrate <vm_name> host=<host_ip>
acli host.add_affinity host=<host_ip> vm=<vm_name>

questions.explanation: The "acli vm.affinity_set" command with the host_list parameter pins a VM to one or more specific hosts. vm.migrate moves a VM once but does not create a persistent affinity rule, and vm.update does not support a preferred_host parameter.

questions.questionText: An administrator wants to enable cluster lockdown to enforce key-based SSH authentication and disable password-based access to all CVMs. Where is this configured?

options.text: Prism Element > Settings > Cluster Lockdown
ncli cluster edit enable-ssh-lockdown=true
/etc/ssh/sshd_config on each CVM manually
acli cluster.update ssh_lockdown=true

questions.explanation: Cluster Lockdown is configured through Prism Element under Settings > Cluster Lockdown, where administrators can disable password-based SSH and upload authorized public keys. Manual sshd_config edits are not supported and would be overwritten.

questions.questionText: An administrator needs to view the full configuration of a specific VM, including vCPU count, memory, attached disks, and NICs. Which command provides this detail?

options.text: acli vm.get <vm_name>
acli vm.list include_details=true
ncli vm info name=<vm_name>
nuclei vm.get <vm_uuid>

questions.explanation: The "acli vm.get <vm_name>" command returns comprehensive VM details including vCPU, memory, disk, and NIC configurations. While nuclei can also retrieve VM info, acli vm.get is the standard AHV CLI approach using the VM name.

questions.questionText: A storage administrator needs to list all storage containers and their current usage statistics. Which command should be used?

options.text: ncli container ls
acli storage.list
ncli storagepool ls
curator_cli display_data

questions.explanation: The "ncli container ls" command lists all storage containers with details including capacity, usage, replication factor, and compression settings. ncli storagepool ls would list storage pools (which aggregate physical disks), not containers.

questions.questionText: An administrator is troubleshooting replication failures between two Nutanix clusters configured with protection domains. Which CVM service is responsible for handling replication?

options.text: Stargate
Cerebro
Curator
Medusa

questions.explanation: Cerebro is the Nutanix service responsible for managing data replication, including protection domain snapshots, remote replication, and disaster recovery operations. Stargate handles local storage I/O, Curator performs background cleanup, and Medusa manages metadata.

questions.questionText: An administrator needs to add a VM named "DB-Server01" to an existing protection domain called "PD-Production." Which command accomplishes this?

options.text: ncli pd add-to-pd name=PD-Production vm-names=DB-Server01
acli vm.update DB-Server01 protection_domain=PD-Production
ncli protection-domain add-vm name=PD-Production vm=DB-Server01
cerebro_cli add_vm pd=PD-Production vm=DB-Server01

questions.explanation: The "ncli pd add-to-pd" command with the name and vm-names parameters adds a VM to an existing protection domain. This is the correct ncli syntax for protection domain management.

questions.questionText: An administrator wants to configure SNMP v3 monitoring integration with SolarWinds for a Nutanix cluster. Which Prism Element path is used to configure the SNMP trap destination?

options.text: Settings > SNMP > Add Transport
Settings > Alerts > SNMP Configuration
Settings > Network > SNMP Trap Receiver
Settings > SNMP > Add Trap Receiver

questions.explanation: The question asks about configuring the SNMP **trap destination**. In Prism Element under Settings > SNMP, there are two sections: "Add Transport" (for SNMP polling/queries) and "Add Trap Receiver" (for sending event notifications to a destination). Since we need the trap destination, the correct path is Settings > SNMP > **Add Trap Receiver**. SNMPv3 requires configuring authentication and privacy protocols within this section.

questions.questionText: An administrator receives an alert about a degraded Cassandra node. Which command displays the metadata store's replication and consistency status?

options.text: medusa_printer --ring_status
nodetool status
cassandra_cli ring_check
ncli alerts ls type=metadata

questions.explanation: The "nodetool status" command (alongside "nodetool ring") provides Cassandra node status including load, state (Up/Down), and ownership percentage. This is the standard diagnostic tool for the Medusa/Cassandra metadata store.

questions.questionText: An administrator needs to create a virtual network with VLAN ID 200 for a new department. Which acli command creates this network?

options.text: acli net.create VLAN200 vlan=200 vswitch_name=vs0
acli net.add name=VLAN200 vlan_id=200
ncli network create name=VLAN200 vlan=200
acli vswitch.add_vlan vs0 vlan=200

questions.explanation: The "acli net.create" command creates a new AHV virtual network with a specified VLAN ID and virtual switch. The syntax requires the network name, VLAN tag, and the virtual switch to bind to.

questions.questionText: During a CVM restart procedure, what must be done FIRST before restarting a CVM to avoid VM disruption?

options.text: Stop all VMs on the host
Put the host into maintenance mode so VMs migrate to other hosts
Stop all CVM services using "cluster stop"
Disable HA on the cluster temporarily

questions.explanation: Before restarting a CVM, the host should be placed into maintenance mode, which triggers live migration of running VMs to other healthy hosts. This ensures zero VM downtime during CVM maintenance.

questions.questionText: An administrator needs to configure syslog forwarding to a Splunk SIEM server at IP 10.0.1.50 on port 514. Where is this configured in Prism?

options.text: Prism Element > Settings > Syslog Server
Prism Element > Settings > SMTP Server
Prism Central > Settings > Log Forwarding
Prism Element > Alerts > External Logging

questions.explanation: Syslog forwarding is configured under Prism Element > Settings > Syslog Server, where administrators specify the remote syslog server IP, port, transport protocol (UDP/TCP/TLS), and log severity levels to forward.

questions.questionText: An administrator is using the v3 API to update an existing VM's memory from 4 GB to 8 GB. What must be included in the PUT request body besides the updated spec?

options.text: Only the changed fields in the spec
The complete spec and the current metadata (including spec_version)
The VM UUID as a query parameter and the new memory value
A PATCH body with only the memory delta change

questions.explanation: The v3 intent-based API requires the full spec and metadata (including spec_version for concurrency control) in PUT requests. The spec_version prevents conflicting updates. Partial updates are not supported—the entire entity definition must be provided.

questions.questionText: An administrator needs to verify the Zeus configuration database on a CVM to check the cluster's stored configuration parameters. Which command displays this information?

options.text: zeus_config_printer
ncli cluster info
cat /home/nutanix/config/zeus.conf
medusa_printer --zeus

questions.explanation: The "zeus_config_printer" command outputs the complete Zeus configuration database contents, which stores cluster-wide configuration parameters replicated across CVMs via Paxos consensus. ncli cluster info shows a subset; zeus_config_printer shows the raw configuration.

questions.questionText: An administrator wants to enable software-based data-at-rest encryption on a specific storage container. What prerequisite must be configured first?

options.text: A Key Management Server (KMS) — either native Prism or external (e.g., SafeNet, Vormetric)
Self-Encrypting Drives (SEDs) in all nodes
FIPS 140-2 mode enabled on all CVMs
A Trusted Platform Module (TPM) installed in each host

questions.explanation: Software-based data-at-rest encryption (AES-256) requires a configured Key Management Server to store and manage encryption keys. Nutanix supports native key management or integration with external KMS solutions. SEDs are only required for hardware-based encryption.

questions.questionText: An administrator is troubleshooting high storage latency and suspects the Stargate service is overwhelmed. Which log file on the CVM should be examined first?

options.text: /home/nutanix/data/logs/stargate.INFO
/var/log/messages
/home/nutanix/data/logs/curator.INFO
/home/nutanix/data/logs/prism_gateway.log

questions.explanation: Stargate logs are located at /home/nutanix/data/logs/stargate.INFO on each CVM. This log contains storage I/O operation details, error conditions, and performance metrics critical for diagnosing storage latency issues.

questions.questionText: An administrator needs to run all NCC (Nutanix Cluster Check) health checks from the command line. Which command executes the full health check suite?

options.text: ncli ncc health_checks run_all
ncc health_checks run_all
ncli cluster run-ncc-checks
ncc --run-all-checks

questions.explanation: The "ncc health_checks run_all" command runs the complete NCC health check suite from the CVM command line. While the question stem mentions ncli, the actual NCC binary is invoked directly as "ncc health_checks run_all" (not through ncli).

questions.questionText: An administrator wants to authenticate to the Nutanix v3 REST API. Which authentication method is required?

options.text: OAuth 2.0 bearer token
Basic authentication with base64-encoded username:password
API key in the X-Nutanix-Api-Key header
SAML assertion token

questions.explanation: The Nutanix v3 REST API uses HTTP Basic authentication, where the username:password string is base64-encoded and sent in the Authorization header. OAuth and API key authentication are not natively supported for the Prism v3 API.

questions.questionText: An administrator notices that Curator scans are taking significantly longer than usual. Which command provides details about Curator's current scan status and operations?

options.text: curator_cli display_data
ncli curator status
genesis status curator
stargate_cli show_curator

questions.explanation: The "curator_cli display_data" command shows Curator's operational data, including scan details, garbage collection status, and disk balancing information. Curator manages background operations like data compaction, tiering, and disk rebalancing.

questions.questionText: An administrator needs to create a snapshot of a VM named "WebApp01" using the AHV command line. Which command creates this snapshot?

options.text: acli snapshot.create WebApp01 snapshot_name=WebApp01-snap1
ncli snapshot create vm-name=WebApp01 name=WebApp01-snap1
acli vm.snapshot_create WebApp01 name=WebApp01-snap1
nuclei snapshot.create vm=WebApp01

questions.explanation: The "acli snapshot.create" command creates a point-in-time snapshot of an AHV VM. The command takes the VM name and an optional snapshot name as parameters for identification.

questions.questionText: An administrator wants to list all current alerts on the cluster, filtered to only show critical severity. Which ncli command accomplishes this?

options.text: ncli alerts ls severity=critical
ncli alert list --filter=critical
ncli alerts ls resolved=false severity=CRITICAL
ncli cluster alerts filter=critical

questions.explanation: The "ncli alerts ls" command supports filtering by severity level (using uppercase values like CRITICAL) and resolved status. Combining resolved=false with severity=CRITICAL shows only active critical alerts requiring attention.

questions.questionText: An administrator is using the v3 API to list VMs and receives a 429 HTTP response code. What does this indicate, and what should the administrator do?

options.text: Authentication failure; re-encode credentials in base64
Rate limit exceeded; implement exponential backoff or use batch API calls
VM not found; verify the UUID is correct
API version mismatch; switch from v3 to v2

questions.explanation: HTTP 429 indicates the API rate limit has been exceeded. Administrators should implement exponential backoff retry logic or use the batch API endpoint for bulk operations to reduce the number of individual API calls.

questions.questionText: An administrator needs to power cycle a VM named "TestVM" that is in an unresponsive state. Which acli command performs a hard power cycle?

options.text: acli vm.power_cycle TestVM
acli vm.reset TestVM
acli vm.power_off TestVM && acli vm.power_on TestVM
acli vm.force_restart TestVM

questions.explanation: The "acli vm.power_cycle" command performs a hard power cycle on the specified VM, equivalent to pressing the physical power button off and on. This is the correct single command for an immediate power cycle.

questions.questionText: An administrator applies STIG hardening to a Nutanix cluster. Which automated tool validates ongoing compliance with the applied security configuration?

options.text: SCMA (Security Configuration Management Automation)
ncc security_checks run_all
ncli cluster check-stig-compliance
Prism Central > Security Dashboard > STIG Validation

questions.explanation: SCMA (Security Configuration Management Automation) is the Nutanix tool that performs automated, scheduled checks against the applied STIG baseline to ensure ongoing compliance. It reports drift from the security configuration and can remediate deviations.

questions.questionText: An administrator needs to replace the default self-signed SSL certificate on Prism Element with a CA-signed certificate. Which ncli command initiates the certificate replacement?

options.text: ncli ssl-certificate replace
ncli cluster set-ssl-certificate
ncli http-proxy update-cert
ncli cluster update-ssl certificate_path=/path/cert.pem

questions.explanation: The certificate replacement process for Prism uses specific ncli commands or the Prism UI under Settings. The "ncli ssl-certificate replace" command (or the equivalent Prism UI flow) is used to install the CA-signed certificate along with the private key and certificate chain.

questions.questionText: An administrator is configuring the Nutanix v3 API to create a category key named "Environment" with values "Production" and "Development." Which API endpoint is used?

options.text: PUT https://prism-central:9440/api/nutanix/v3/categories/Environment
POST https://prism-central:9440/api/nutanix/v3/categories
PUT https://prism-central:9440/api/nutanix/v3/categories/Environment/Production
POST https://prism-central:9440/api/nutanix/v3/category/create

questions.explanation: In the v3 API, category values are set by making PUT requests to /categories/{key}/{value}. The category key must be created first via PUT /categories/{key}, then individual values are added with PUT /categories/{key}/{value}.

questions.questionText: An administrator needs to check the status of all CVM services on the local CVM. Which command shows the running state of each service?

options.text: genesis status
cluster status
systemctl list-units --type=service
ncli cluster get-service-status

questions.explanation: The "cluster status" command run from any CVM shows the status of all CVM services (Stargate, Prism, Curator, Cerebro, etc.) across the entire cluster. "genesis status" shows services on the local CVM only, while "cluster status" provides the cluster-wide view.

questions.questionText: An administrator needs to configure data-at-rest encryption cluster-wide using software encryption. Which encryption algorithm does Nutanix use for software-based encryption?

options.text: AES-256
AES-128
3DES

questions.explanation: Nutanix software-based data-at-rest encryption uses the AES-256 (Advanced Encryption Standard with 256-bit keys) algorithm. This is applied at the storage container or cluster level and requires a configured KMS for key management.

questions.questionText: An administrator is troubleshooting network connectivity for a VM and needs to list all virtual networks configured on the AHV cluster. Which command should be used?

options.text: acli net.list
ncli network list
acli vswitch.list
ovs-vsctl show

questions.explanation: The "acli net.list" command lists all AHV virtual networks with their VLAN IDs, UUID, and virtual switch assignments. While "ovs-vsctl show" shows Open vSwitch configuration, "acli net.list" is the proper Nutanix abstraction for network management.

questions.questionText: An administrator needs to troubleshoot replication schedule failures for a protection domain. Which log file on the CVM contains Cerebro service logs?

options.text: /home/nutanix/data/logs/cerebro.INFO
/home/nutanix/data/logs/replication.log
/var/log/cerebro/cerebro.log
/home/nutanix/data/logs/stargate_replication.INFO

questions.explanation: Cerebro service logs are located at /home/nutanix/data/logs/cerebro.INFO. This log file contains details about replication operations, protection domain schedules, snapshot management, and remote site connectivity.

questions.questionText: An administrator wants to update an existing VM's vCPU count from 2 to 4 using acli. The VM is named "AppServer." Which command modifies the vCPU configuration?

options.text: acli vm.update AppServer num_vcpus=4
acli vm.cpu_update AppServer vcpus=4
acli vm.set AppServer cpu_count=4
ncli vm update name=AppServer vcpus=4

questions.explanation: The "acli vm.update" command is used to modify VM configurations including vCPU count, memory, and other settings. The num_vcpus parameter specifies the desired number of virtual CPUs. The VM must be powered off for CPU changes to take effect.

questions.questionText: An administrator receives an HTTP 409 Conflict response when updating a VM via the v3 API. What is the most likely cause?

options.text: The VM UUID does not exist
The spec_version in the request body is outdated due to a concurrent modification
The API rate limit has been exceeded
The request body contains invalid JSON formatting

questions.explanation: HTTP 409 Conflict in the v3 API indicates a spec_version mismatch, meaning the entity was modified between the GET and PUT operations. The administrator must retrieve the latest spec_version with a new GET request before retrying the update.

questions.questionText: An administrator needs to verify that all nodes in the cluster are healthy and participating. Which ncli command lists all hosts with their status?

options.text: ncli host ls
ncli cluster info
ncli node list
ncli hypervisor ls

questions.explanation: The "ncli host ls" command lists all hosts in the cluster with details including hostname, IP address, CVM IP, hypervisor type, and current status (normal, degraded, etc.).

questions.questionText: An administrator wants to use the v3 API to retrieve a list of VMs with filtering. Which API call retrieves VMs filtered by a specific category assignment?

options.text: GET https://prism-central:9440/api/nutanix/v3/vms?category=Production
POST https://prism-central:9440/api/nutanix/v3/vms/list with filter criteria in body
GET https://prism-central:9440/api/nutanix/v3/vms/list?filter=category==Production
POST https://prism-central:9440/api/nutanix/v3/categories/Production/vms

questions.explanation: The v3 API uses POST to /vms/list with filter criteria specified in the JSON request body (including kind, offset, length, and filter fields). This intent-based design differs from traditional REST where GET with query parameters would be used for listing.

questions.questionText: An administrator needs to identify which CVM service manages the distributed configuration database and uses Paxos consensus for replication. Which service is this?

options.text: Zeus
Medusa
Cerebro
Prism
